skill.name: web-performance-optimization
skill.description: Guides developers through web performance audits, Core Web Vitals improvements, bundle reduction, asset optimization, and verification workflows.
skill.summary: Improve page speed, Core Web Vitals, bundle size, images, caching, and performance verification.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium indicators, but most are false positives caused by markdown examples rather than executable skill code. The remaining confirmed concern is moderate: the documentation includes npm, npx, Lighthouse, dynamic import, URL, and filesystem examples that an agent or user may execute while changing a project.

medium_findings.title: Project-Mutating Package Commands in Examples
medium_findings.description: The skill includes npm uninstall, npm install, npx, and Lighthouse commands in markdown examples. These are legitimate performance workflows, but they can modify dependencies or execute local tooling if followed automatically.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly present and would affect a project if executed. They appear intended for performance optimization, not malicious activity.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Batch Processing Example
medium_findings.description: The image optimization example reads files from ./images and writes optimized images with sharp. This is appropriate for the skill, but it can create or overwrite generated assets if run without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem access is explicit in the example code and has a plausible benign purpose. The risk depends on whether an agent executes the snippet in a user repository.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Import Findings Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The dynamic import() findings occur in React and Next.js code-splitting examples. They are not executed by the skill and do not load remote code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The imports are inside markdown examples that demonstrate lazy loading. No evidence found that the skill executes these imports.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Findings Are Benign References or Placeholders
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are a placeholder analytics script, a sample Lighthouse target, and documentation links. No evidence found of credential exfiltration or unauthorized callbacks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible examples and public documentation links. They do not receive secrets or hidden data in the skill content.

low_findings.title: High Static Blocker Hits Are Text-Match False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance hits are caused by ordinary documentation words and performance guidance. No evidence found of cryptographic algorithms, system reconnaissance, or network scanning logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found normal prose and HTML attributes at the cited lines. No evidence found for weak cryptography or reconnaissance behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Executable Shell Commands in Markdown Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill provides shell commands for dependency changes, bundle analysis, image tooling installation, and Lighthouse audits. These should require explicit user approval before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is present in documentation examples and can change the local project. The commands are standard development tools, so intent appears benign.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local File Read and Asset Write Example
dangerous_patterns.description: The image optimization snippet reads image filenames and writes converted assets. It is useful guidance, but it touches local files when executed.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code shows local filesystem use through Node.js fs and sharp output calls. It is confined to an image optimization example.

dangerous_patterns.title: Deferred Third-Party Script Loading Example
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill shows a placeholder third-party analytics script loaded after page load. This is a performance pattern, but third-party scripts should be reviewed for privacy and supply-chain impact.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The example clearly adds an external script URL. The domain is a generic placeholder, so there is no evidence of malicious exfiltration.

content.user_title: Optimize Web Performance and Core Web Vitals
content.value_statement: Slow pages reduce conversions, search visibility, and user trust. This skill gives a structured workflow for audits, fixes, and verification.
actual_capabilities: Defines a measurement-first workflow for Lighthouse audits and Core Web Vitals baselines.
Helps prioritize LCP, FID, CLS, TTI, TTFB, and bundle-size issues.
Suggests JavaScript bundle reductions through analysis, code splitting, and dependency replacement.
Provides image optimization guidance for WebP, AVIF, responsive images, and lazy loading.
Covers caching, CDN usage, preloading, and post-change verification checklists.
limitations: It does not run audits or collect metrics without a connected tool or user-provided data.
The examples are general and must be adapted to each framework and deployment stack.
Some recommendations require package installs, project changes, or external performance tools.
It does not provide a ready-made monitoring integration or production dashboard.

use_cases.title: Improve a Slow Frontend Release
use_cases.description: Use the skill to identify large bundles, blocking scripts, image issues, and layout shifts before release.

use_cases.title: Prepare for SEO and Core Web Vitals Reviews
use_cases.description: Use the workflow to translate Lighthouse and PageSpeed results into clear engineering priorities.

use_cases.title: Create a Performance Remediation Plan
use_cases.description: Use the checklists to scope fixes, assign work, and verify improvements across mobile and desktop.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Performance Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my page performance data and identify the top three issues affecting load speed and Core Web Vitals.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Core Web Vitals
prompt_templates.prompt: Given these LCP, FID, CLS, TTFB, and Lighthouse results, create a prioritized optimization plan with expected impact.

prompt_templates.title: Reduce JavaScript Bundle Size
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my bundle report and recommend dependency, code splitting, lazy loading, and tree-shaking improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Release Performance Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a performance budget, verification checklist, and monitoring plan for a production web application release.

output_examples.input: A page has slow LCP, high CLS, and a large JavaScript bundle.
output: A prioritized audit summary that separates image, layout, and JavaScript problems.
A remediation plan focused on hero image optimization, stable dimensions, and code splitting.
A verification checklist using before-and-after Lighthouse and real user metrics.

output_examples.input: A team wants to reduce load time on mobile networks.
output: A mobile-first optimization plan for images, scripts, fonts, caching, and server response time.
A suggested performance budget for bundle size, LCP, CLS, and TTFB.
A short list of tools for measurement and ongoing monitoring.

output_examples.input: A bundle analysis shows large date, utility, and charting dependencies.
output: A dependency review that ranks replacement, selective import, lazy loading, and route splitting options.
A risk note for changes that need regression testing.
A final validation plan for bundle size and user interaction metrics.

best_practices: Measure baseline performance before changing code, assets, or infrastructure.
Prioritize user-visible metrics such as LCP, CLS, TTFB, and interaction delay.
Verify improvements on mobile devices, slow networks, and production-like builds.
anti_patterns: Installing or removing dependencies before confirming the performance problem.
Optimizing desktop scores while ignoring mobile network and CPU limits.
Relying on one Lighthouse run without comparing real user metrics or repeated tests.

faq.question: Does this skill run Lighthouse automatically?
faq.answer: No. It explains how to use Lighthouse and related tools, but execution depends on the connected environment.

faq.question: Can it help with Core Web Vitals?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers LCP, FID, CLS, TTFB, and TTI diagnosis with practical remediation steps.

faq.question: Is it only for React or Next.js?
faq.answer: No. Some examples use React and Next.js, but the performance workflow applies to many web stacks.

faq.question: Will it change my project dependencies?
faq.answer: Only if you or an agent choose to run the documented npm commands. Review dependency changes before applying them.

faq.question: Can it optimize images?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains modern formats, responsive images, lazy loading, and batch conversion concepts.

faq.question: Does it include production monitoring?
faq.answer: It recommends monitoring tools and metrics, but it does not include a complete monitoring integration.
